narrative_blocks.header: Plaintiff in 1943 Georgia Supreme Court estate suit
narrative_blocks.body: Edward Werner Jr. was named plaintiff in the Georgia Supreme Court 1943 estate case Werner versus Werner — number 14530 — in which he sued his brothers Lynn and Ray and sister Florence Brine over their parents' estate. Edward alleged that brother Lynn had mismanaged the estate and appropriated profits from the sale of 528 West Peachtree Street Atlanta. The reported case became part of the published record of mid-twentieth-century Georgia probate law.

narrative_blocks.header: Two marriages and three documented children
narrative_blocks.body: Edward had two marriages with three documented children. His wife Elizabeth Weakley bore daughters Elisabeth Werner born 1908 and Harriette Werner born 1915; his wife Ethel Margaret Cook of Atlanta bore daughter Elizabeth C. Werner born 1907. The chronological ordering of the two marriages remains unsettled across descendant records.

Edward Werner Jr. was born September 5, 1874 in Huntsville, Madison County Alabama and died May 24, 1955 in Atlanta, Fulton County Georgia at age eighty. He is buried at Oakland Cemetery Atlanta — one of the most prominent nineteenth-century Southern garden cemeteries, holding the graves of Margaret Mitchell, Bobby Jones, and many of Atlanta's founding families.

narrative_blocks.header: Hooker descent through mother Florence Cowles Werner
narrative_blocks.body: Edward inherited Hooker descent through his mother Florence Woodruff Cowles Werner — the Connecticut Cowles family being one of the major mid-nineteenth-century New England-to-Deep South migration lines that brought Connecticut Yankees south of the Mason-Dixon. His father Edward Alphonso Werner Sr. was a non-Hooker line spouse.

sources.short: Werner v. Werner, 195 Ga. 868 (1943) — named plaintiff

burialCemetery.name: Oakland Cemetery
burialCemetery.notes: Historic Atlanta cemetery founded 1850. Burial place of the Werner-Cowles family branch: Florence Woodruff Cowles Werner (HD3774), her husband Edward A. Werner Sr (I00425), and all four of their children — Edward Jr (HD3775), Lynn Woodruff (HD3776), Florence Brine (HD3777), Ray Cowles (HD3778) — plus Ray's wife Gertrude Comly Werner (I00427). Contains notable Atlanta citizens including 6 governors, Margaret Mitchell, Bobby Jones.
